applicant_history.applicants_experience_with_haunted_house:
The applicant, Black Hollow Productions, LLC (managing member: Jonathan "Jon" Keller), has operated seasonal haunted attractions continuously since 2014. The primary attraction is "Black Hollow Haunted Mill," located at a repurposed textile mill at 219 Old Foundry Road, Lancaster, PA 17602.

Timeline and scope of experience:
- 2011–2013 (informal experience): Jon Keller volunteered and later worked part‑time at "Night Terrors Haunted Hayride" in York, PA, assisting with set building, actor coordination, and queue line management. This was not under Black Hollow Productions but provided 3 seasons of prior industry experience.

- 2014 season (first year as operator):
  • Opened "Black Hollow Haunted Mill" for the first time under Black Hollow Productions, LLC.
  • Operating dates: 2014-10-03 through 2014-11-01 (Friday–Sunday, plus Halloween week Thursday).
  • Approx. 12 event nights.
  • Single attraction: 12,000 sq. ft. indoor walkthrough plus outdoor queue line.

- 2015–2017 seasons (expansion period):
  • 2015 operating dates: 2015-10-02 through 2015-10-31, 14 event nights.
  • 2016 operating dates: 2016-09-30 through 2016-10-31, 15 event nights.
  • 2017 operating dates: 2017-09-29 through 2017-11-04, 16 event nights.
  • During this period they expanded from 1 to 2 attractions:
     - "Black Hollow Haunted Mill" (indoor walkthrough, multi-level).
     - "Cornfield of Shadows" (outdoor trail added in 2016 on leased farmland adjacent to the mill).
  • Average annual attendance: approx. 6,500–8,000 guests.

- 2018–2020 seasons (multi‑attraction operation, added escape room):
  • 2018 operating dates: 2018-09-28 through 2018-11-03, 17 event nights.
  • 2019 operating dates: 2019-09-27 through 2019-11-02, 18 event nights.
  • 2020 operating dates (reduced due to COVID‑19): 2020-10-02 through 2020-10-31, 10 event nights with timed ticketing and reduced capacity.
  • Attractions operated:
     - "Black Hollow Haunted Mill" (flagship walkthrough, approx. 18,000 sq. ft.).
     - "Cornfield of Shadows" (outdoor trail, approx. 3/4 mile path).
     - "Panic Room 13" (small-group escape room experience, added 2018, operated year‑round on weekends but with a Halloween‑themed overlay during October).
  • During 2020, the outdoor trail and escape room were operated with strict capacity, distancing, and mask requirements in line with local health guidance.

- 2021–2024 seasons (current operations and refinement):
  • 2021 operating dates: 2021-10-01 through 2021-11-06, 15 event nights.
  • 2022 operating dates: 2022-09-30 through 2022-11-05, 16 event nights.
  • 2023 operating dates: 2023-09-29 through 2023-11-04, 17 event nights.
  • Planned 2024 operating dates: 2024-09-27 through 2024-11-02, approximately 15–16 event nights (Friday–Sunday, plus Halloween week Thursday).
  • Current attractions:
     - "Black Hollow Haunted Mill" (primary indoor walkthrough, 20,000+ sq. ft., fire‑sprinklered, multiple emergency exits, ADA‑accessible modified route).
     - "Cornfield of Shadows" (outdoor haunted trail; max group size 8, no open flames, supervised by staff with radios and flashlights).
     - "Panic Room 13" escape room (booked separately year‑round, capacity 6 guests per 60‑minute session).
     - Seasonal add‑ons: photo op area, concession stand, and covered queue line entertainment with roaming actors.

Operational experience details:
- Years of operation as owner/operator: 10 completed seasons (2014–2023), entering the 11th season in 2024.
- Number of locations managed: primarily 1 physical property (the mill and adjoining leased cornfield), with the escape room built into a separate wing of the same building. No traveling/portable haunts.
- Average annual attendance (last 3 completed seasons):
  • 2021: approx. 9,200 guests.
  • 2022: approx. 10,100 guests.
  • 2023: approx. 10,850 guests.

Safety and training:
- All actors and staff (typically 65–80 seasonal workers) complete mandatory safety orientation each season, including:
  • Emergency evacuation procedures and exit routes.
  • No‑contact rules (no intentional physical contact with guests; no grabbing or blocking exits).
  • Use of props and power tools (only trained build crew may use powered equipment; no real weapons allowed).
  • Trip/fall hazard identification and daily walkthrough checklists.
- The applicant maintains written incident logs each night, documenting any first aid rendered, guest complaints, or near‑miss events.
- The attraction has passed annual local fire marshal inspections since 2014 and adheres to occupancy limits and exit signage requirements.

In summary, Black Hollow Productions, LLC has a decade of continuous experience operating a multi‑attraction haunted event at a single, fixed location in Lancaster, PA, with growing attendance and a structured approach to safety, training, and risk management.

Contact information for context:
- Managing Member: Jonathan Keller
- Business Address: 219 Old Foundry Road, Suite B, Lancaster, PA 17602
- Phone: (717) 555-2947
- Email: jon.keller@blackhollowhaunt.com

premium_and_loss_record_table.non_renewal_or_cancel: Policy renewed; minor medical payment claim only, no cancellation or non‑renewal.

applicant_history.details_of_losses_incidents_for_the_past:
Within the past five years, the applicant has had one reportable loss that resulted in a paid claim under the general liability/medical payments portion of the policy, along with several minor incidents that were documented internally but did not result in claims.

1) 2022 minor trip‑and‑fall incident – paid med pay claim
- Date of incident: 2022-10-15
- Location: Interior stairwell area of "Black Hollow Haunted Mill," midway through the attraction.
- Description of circumstances:
  • A female guest, approximately 32 years old, was walking down a short flight of three steps in a dimly lit transition hallway. The area was equipped with step edge marking tape and low‑level lighting, but the guest reported that she was distracted by her friend behind her and did not fully see the last step.
  • The guest misjudged the final step, twisted her left ankle, and sat down immediately, reporting pain but remaining conscious and alert. No contact by actors or staff was involved.
  • Staff response: A nearby actor called for a floor supervisor via handheld radio. The show in that section was temporarily paused, and lights were raised. The on‑site first aid attendant (who holds a current basic first aid/CPR certification) evaluated the guest. Ice was applied and the ankle was elevated in a quiet area near the staff break room.
  • The guest was able to bear some weight but requested to leave and have the ankle checked at urgent care. Her friend drove her; no ambulance was requested.

- Reporting and follow‑up:
  • An incident report form was completed the same night by the floor supervisor (including diagrams, witness statements from the guest’s friend and one nearby guest, and photos of the stair area and lighting).
  • Management followed up with the guest by phone the next day. She reported that urgent care diagnosed a mild ankle sprain, recommended rest/ice/compression, and provided a small ankle brace.
  • The claim was submitted to the then‑current carrier, MidAtlantic Event & Entertainment Insurance, under the medical payments coverage.

- Claim details:
  • Carrier: MidAtlantic Event & Entertainment Insurance
  • Policy period: 2022-09-01 to 2023-09-01
  • Total paid: approximately $750 (urgent care visit, ankle brace, and follow‑up visit co‑pay).
  • No litigation was threatened or initiated. The guest expressed appreciation for the quick response and follow‑up.

- Corrective measures taken:
  • Additional anti‑slip tread strips were installed on the stair treads.
  • The step edge marking tape was replaced with a wider, higher‑contrast photoluminescent tape.
  • A small low‑level LED strip light was added at the bottom step to better define the change in elevation while still preserving the haunt’s low‑light atmosphere.
  • Staff were reminded during a mid‑season safety briefing to monitor any areas where guests naturally slow down or turn and to report any concerns about visibility or crowding.

2) 2021 queue line fainting episode – no claim filed
- Date of incident: 2021-10-22
- Location: Outdoor covered queue area in front of "Black Hollow Haunted Mill."
- Description:
  • A male guest in his late teens became lightheaded and briefly fainted while waiting in line with friends. Weather was unseasonably warm and humid, and the guest later reported he had not eaten much that day.
  • Staff response: Queue attendants and a roaming actor assisted the guest to a seated position, provided water, and called the on‑site first aid attendant. The guest regained consciousness almost immediately and was alert and oriented.
  • EMS was offered but declined by the guest and his parents (who were reached by phone). They chose to leave the property and monitor him at home.
- Outcome:
  • No medical treatment beyond on‑site first aid was known or reported.
  • No insurance claim was filed. The incident was logged internally with a completed incident report form.
- Corrective actions:
  • Additional signage was added encouraging guests to hydrate and eat before attending.
  • A small fan and misting unit were added for use on unusually warm nights.

3) 2020 minor prop‑related abrasion – no claim filed
- Date of incident: 2020-10-17
- Location: "Cornfield of Shadows" outdoor trail.
- Description:
  • A guest brushed against a wooden fence post that had a small rough edge, resulting in a superficial scratch on the forearm (no significant bleeding).
  • Staff response: The guest was escorted to the first aid station where the area was cleaned with antiseptic wipes and covered with a bandage. The guest continued through the attraction and completed the evening without further issue.
- Outcome:
  • No further medical treatment was sought to the applicant’s knowledge.
  • No claim was filed; the guest signed the incident log noting that they were satisfied with the care provided.
- Corrective actions:
  • The specific fence section was immediately sanded and re‑sealed the next day.
  • A mid‑season inspection of all guest‑accessible wood railings and posts was conducted to identify and correct any similar rough edges.

Other notes:
- The applicant has had no fire, structural, or major crowd‑control incidents; no evacuations other than short, precautionary pauses for medical checks and one brief weather‑related delay for lightning in 2023 (no injuries or damages).
- There have been no assault, battery, or actor‑versus‑patron claims. The operation enforces a strict no‑touch policy for actors and a code of conduct for guests (including removal of guests who attempt to strike actors or damage sets).
- There have been no property damage claims submitted to carriers in the last five years related to storms, vandalism, or equipment failure.

In summary, over the past five years the applicant has experienced one minor medical payments claim (ankle sprain) and a small number of minor incidents handled entirely on‑site, with documented corrective actions and enhanced safety measures implemented after each event.
